questions.question: What is the Ethereum network and how does it differ from other blockchain platforms?
keyPoints: Decentralized, open-source blockchain platform
Enables creation of smart contracts and decentralized applications
Uses proof-of-work consensus mechanism

questions.question: Describe the different layers of the Ethereum stack and their functions.
keyPoints: Network Layer: connects nodes and transmits data
Data Layer: stores and manages data on the blockchain
Consensus Layer: achieves consensus among nodes
Application Layer: executes smart contracts and decentralized applications

questions.question: What is gas in the context of the Ethereum network and how is it used?
keyPoints: Unit of measurement for computational effort
Required to execute transactions and smart contracts
Used to prevent spam and denial-of-service attacks

questions.question: Explain the concept of the world state in the context of the Ethereum network and how it is updated.
keyPoints: Current state of the Ethereum network
Includes all accounts, balances, and smart contracts
Updated with each new block added to the blockchain

questions.question: What is the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) and what is its purpose?
keyPoints: Turing-complete machine for executing smart contracts
Used to execute smart contracts on the Ethereum network
Provides a sandboxed environment for contract execution

questions.question: Describe the different types of transactions on the Ethereum network and their purposes.
keyPoints: Contract creation transactions: create new smart contracts
Message call transactions: execute functions on smart contracts
Value transfer transactions: transfer Ether between accounts

questions.question: What is the difference between a hard fork and a soft fork in the context of the Ethereum network?
keyPoints: Hard fork: non-backward compatible change to the protocol
Soft fork: backward compatible change to the protocol
